summary: Thailand's transition is led by the National Cyber Security Agency through its QUANTA national readiness platform, which now publishes captured guidance, a recommended-standards page and a phased migration roadmap to educate public and private organisations and provide tools and labs for migration. The agency recommends the NIST post-quantum standards, naming FIPS 203 (ML-KEM) for key encapsulation, FIPS 204 (ML-DSA) for digital signatures and FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA) for long-term archival, and sets out a phased roadmap aiming to complete the national transition to quantum-resistant cryptography by 2035. The guidance is advisory rather than a legal obligation, and the Securities and Exchange Commission has begun raising quantum risk with the capital market.
migrationTimeline:
2025 | Discovery and risk assessment of cryptographic estate
2027 | Post-quantum cryptography integration
2031 | Full system migration
2035 | National transition to quantum-resistant cryptography complete
govActors: NCSA | National Cyber Security Agency, runs the QUANTA national post-quantum readiness platform and the migration roadmap
mainRegulation: NCSA QUANTA recommended post-quantum cryptography standards and migration roadmap | national | guidance
